scope: Hong Kong DSE Biology curriculum (S4-S6)
Compulsory part (4 topics) and elective part (choose 2 of 4 topics)
Public exam: Paper 1 (compulsory), Paper 2 (elective), SBA (practical/inquiry skills)
out_of_scope: Non-DSE biology curricula unless explicitly compared
University-level biology
Teacher pedagogy, school administration, resource selection
classification_hints: Use this root only when no more specific topic fits
Prefer compulsory/elective subdivision nodes for broad queries

scope: Four compulsory topics assessed in Paper 1
Approximately 200 curriculum hours
out_of_scope: Elective topics (Paper 2 only)
School-based assessment practical instructions as standalone content
classification_hints: Route ordinary biology content here unless it clearly matches an elective
Use the specific topic node, not this parent, for final classification

scope: Life molecules: water, inorganic ions, carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, nucleic acids
Cell organisation: discovery of cells, cell membrane, subcellular structures, prokaryotic vs eukaryotic cells
Membrane transport: diffusion, osmosis, active transport, endocytosis, plasmolysis, haemolysis
Cell cycle and division: mitosis and meiosis
Cell energetics: metabolism, enzymes, photosynthesis, respiration (aerobic and anaerobic)
out_of_scope: Detailed molecular mechanisms beyond syllabus statements
Plant/animal whole-organism physiology (covered in Organisms and Environment)
classification_hints: Look for keywords: cell membrane, chloroplast, mitochondria, enzyme, photosynthesis, respiration, mitosis, meiosis
Items about biochemical pathways at cellular level belong here
common_misconceptions: Thinking photosynthesis and respiration are reverse processes in every detail

common_misclassifications.reason: Photosynthesis may be mentioned, but whole-plant gas exchange and transpiration are in Organisms and Environment.

overlap_links.notes: Cellular photosynthesis underpins plant gas exchange and transpiration.
Cellular respiration underpins digestion and gas exchange.

scope: Basic genetics: Mendelian laws, human inheritance, pedigree analysis, variation
Molecular genetics: chromosomes, genes, DNA, protein synthesis, mutations, recombinant DNA, DNA fingerprinting, Human Genome Project
Biodiversity and evolution: diversity of life forms, classification (six kingdoms, three domains), origin of life, evolution, speciation, evidence
out_of_scope: Population genetics mathematics beyond syllabus
Detailed bioengineering protocols (covered in elective Bioengineering)
classification_hints: Look for keywords: gene, allele, chromosome, DNA, RNA, mutation, pedigree, natural selection, speciation, classification
Items about inheritance patterns or molecular genetics belong here

common_misclassifications.reason: Recombinant DNA and DNA fingerprinting appear here in compulsory molecular genetics, but detailed biotech processes and ethics are in the elective.

overlap_links.notes: Molecular genetics techniques lead into elective bioengineering.

scope: Plant life processes: nutrition, gas exchange, transpiration, transport, support
Animal life processes: human nutrition, digestion, gas exchange, transport (circulatory and lymphatic)
Reproduction, growth and development: asexual and sexual reproduction in plants and humans, growth curves
Coordination and response: nervous coordination, hormonal coordination, musculoskeletal movement
Homeostasis: temperature regulation, osmoregulation, blood glucose regulation
Ecosystems: energy flow, nutrient cycling, food chains/webs, ecological succession, human impact
out_of_scope: Elective-level physiology (Human Physiology elective)
Elective-level applied ecology (Applied Ecology elective)
classification_hints: Look for keywords: transpiration, digestion, gas exchange, circulation, nervous system, hormone, homeostasis, food chain, ecosystem
Whole-organism physiology and ecology belong here
common_misconceptions: Thinking all hormones act slowly and all nerve impulses act fast in every situation

common_misclassifications.reason: Basic human coordination and homeostasis are compulsory; detailed renal, thermoregulatory and cardiovascular control are elective.
Basic ecosystems and human impact are compulsory; pollution control, conservation strategies and global issues are elective.

overlap_links.notes: Compulsory coordination/homeostasis extends into elective human physiology.
Compulsory ecosystems extend into elective applied ecology.

scope: Plant nutrition and mineral requirements
Photosynthesis at organism level
Gas exchange in plants
Transpiration and factors affecting rate
Transport of water, minerals and organic nutrients
Plant support: turgor and xylem mechanics
out_of_scope: Cellular photosynthesis details (Cells and Molecules)
classification_hints: Keywords: root hair, xylem, phloem, stomata, transpiration, translocation, support

common_misclassifications.reason: Photosynthesis light reactions and Calvin cycle are cellular; leaf gas exchange and transpiration are organism-level.

scope: Human nutrition: food substances, balanced diet, ingestion, digestion, absorption, assimilation, egestion
Human gas exchange: respiratory system, alveoli, ventilation, gas transport
Transport: circulatory system, lymphatic system, tissue fluid, blood components
out_of_scope: Cellular respiration details (Cells and Molecules)
classification_hints: Keywords: digestion, alveoli, ventilation, heart, blood, lymph, tissue fluid

common_misclassifications.reason: Respiration pathways are cellular; breathing and circulation are organism-level.

scope: Asexual reproduction: binary fission, vegetative propagation
Sexual reproduction in flowering plants: flower structure, pollination, fertilisation, seed/fruit dispersal
Human reproduction: male/female systems, menstrual cycle, fertilisation, placenta, birth, parental care
Growth and development: parameters, growth curves, seed germination
out_of_scope: Hormonal control of menstrual cycle (elective Human Physiology)
classification_hints: Keywords: pollination, fertilisation, placenta, menstrual cycle, growth curve, germination

common_misclassifications.reason: Reproductive hormones as control of menstrual cycle is elective; basic reproductive structures and cycles are compulsory.

scope: Nervous coordination: neurones, reflex arc, reflex and voluntary actions
Hormonal coordination: endocrine system, examples of hormonal regulation
Musculoskeletal system: bones, muscles, joints, movement
out_of_scope: Detailed feedback mechanisms of temperature/osmoregulation (elective)
classification_hints: Keywords: neurone, synapse, reflex arc, endocrine, hormone, joint, muscle

common_misclassifications.reason: Basic nervous/hormonal coordination is compulsory; detailed homeostatic control mechanisms are elective.

scope: Temperature regulation
Osmoregulation (basic)
Blood glucose regulation
out_of_scope: Detailed renal physiology and ADH mechanism (elective Human Physiology)
classification_hints: Keywords: negative feedback, thermoregulation, osmoregulation, blood glucose

common_misclassifications.reason: Basic homeostasis is compulsory; detailed kidney nephron and ADH are elective.

scope: Ecological surveying: quadrats, transects, sampling
Energy flow: food chains, food webs, pyramids of numbers and biomass, energy transfer efficiency
Nutrient cycling
Ecological succession: primary and secondary, climax community
Human impact on ecosystems (introductory)
out_of_scope: Detailed pollution control and conservation strategies (elective Applied Ecology)
classification_hints: Keywords: food chain, food web, pyramid, succession, ecosystem, population, community

common_misclassifications.reason: Basic ecology is compulsory; pollution control, resource management and global issues are elective.

scope: Personal health: meaning of health
Disease: infectious vs non-infectious diseases, examples, transmission routes, treatment, antibiotics, vaccination
Body defence mechanisms: non-specific and specific immunity, antigen-antibody reactions, primary and secondary responses, active and passive immunity
out_of_scope: Detailed immunology beyond syllabus statements
Medical diagnosis protocols
classification_hints: Look for keywords: infectious disease, non-infectious disease, immunity, antibody, antigen, vaccination, antibiotic

common_misclassifications.reason: Microbes as pathogens are compulsory; microbial growth, food microbiology and genetic engineering are elective.

overlap_links.notes: Pathogens introduced here; elective extends to microbial techniques and applications.

scope: Four elective topics; students choose 2
Assessed in Paper 2 only
out_of_scope: Compulsory part content (Paper 1)
classification_hints: Route items here only if they match one of the four electives
If a topic could be compulsory or elective, prefer the elective only when the depth matches Paper 2 scope

topics.topic_name: V. Human Physiology: Regulation and Control
scope: Osmoregulation: kidney structure, nephron, urine formation, ADH, dialysis
Thermoregulation: skin, hypothalamus, circulation, thyroid hormone, muscle, behaviour
Blood gas regulation: respiratory centre, chemoreceptors, CO2, cardiac output control
Reproductive hormone control: menstrual cycle hormones, contraception, infertility treatment
out_of_scope: Basic compulsory coordination and homeostasis unless applied at elective depth
classification_hints: Keywords: nephron, ADH, dialysis, hypothalamus, thermoregulation, chemoreceptor, cardiac output, menstrual cycle, contraception

common_misclassifications.reason: Both cover temperature and osmoregulation, but this elective treats renal and cardiovascular control in depth.

scope: Human impact on environment: population growth, resource use, urbanisation, industrialisation
Pollution control: 3Rs, sewage treatment
Conservation: biodiversity, endangered species, habitats, ecological restoration
Global issues: sustainable development, resource management, global warming, acid rain, eutrophication
out_of_scope: Basic ecosystems content unless applied to human impact
classification_hints: Keywords: pollution, conservation, sustainable development, global warming, acid rain, eutrophication, sewage treatment

common_misclassifications.reason: Both involve ecology; Applied Ecology focuses on human impacts and management strategies.

scope: Microbiology: viruses, microbial diversity, microbial growth, aseptic technique
Use of microbes: food processing, vaccines, antibiotics, industrial enzymes, sewage treatment, biogas
Microbial genetics: genetically modified microbes
Harmful microbes: disease, food poisoning, biodeterioration, microbial growth control
out_of_scope: Compulsory disease/immunity content unless at microbial technique depth
classification_hints: Keywords: bacteria, virus, yeast, aseptic technique, fermentation, antibiotic, GM microbe, food poisoning

common_misclassifications.reason: Both discuss pathogens and antibiotics; this elective adds microbial cultivation and industrial applications.

scope: Modern biotech techniques: recombinant DNA, PCR, DNA fingerprinting, GMOs, animal cloning, plant tissue culture
Applications: pharmaceuticals, gene therapy, stem cells, transgenic organisms
Bioethics: moral, legal, social, economic and environmental issues
out_of_scope: Compulsory molecular genetics basics unless applied as biotech technique
classification_hints: Keywords: recombinant DNA, PCR, gel electrophoresis, GMO, clone, gene therapy, stem cell, bioethics

common_misclassifications.reason: Both involve DNA; Bioengineering focuses on techniques and applications at elective depth.
